title: Scheduling, Affinity, Taints, Topology, Priority, Preemption, and Eviction · Cluster Case: K8s Priority Preemption Eviction
summary: A critical workload remains Pending despite free aggregate capacity and later concentrates replicas into one failure domain while lower-priority work is disrupted. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s priority preemption eviction increment in placement and disruption decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.
Calculate eligible domains, global minimum, maxSkew, minDomains, selectors, node inclusion policies, and DoNotSchedule or ScheduleAnyway behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 3. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName. Competency probe: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness. A defensible solution applies K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to placement and disruption decision record.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-scheduling-placement-eviction-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-scheduling-placement-eviction-5
    artifact: placement-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s scheduling placement eviction 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      node-affinity: trusted-workload-class
      toleration: dedicated=intake:NoSchedule
      topologySpread: zones-maxSkew-1
      priorityClass: intake-critical
      preemption-policy: evidence-gated
      eviction: graceful-and-budgeted
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o wide && kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=status-api
    failure: Reject toleration-as-attraction, preferred-as-required, replica count as spread proof, or priority as permission for unbounded disruption.
    repair: Derive eligible nodes and domains, calculate skew, inspect unschedulable reasons, separate preemption from eviction, and preserve graceful recovery.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A higher priority Pod immediately and safely evicts any lower priority Pod until it runs. This fails the K8s Priority Preemption Eviction contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

  - competency: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction
    artifact: placement-policy.yaml
    decision: priority preemption eviction

    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: xpbl5b
    change: |
      node-affinity: trusted-workload-class

      topologySpread: zones-maxSkew-1
      priorityClass: intake-critical
      preemption-policy: evidence-gated
      eviction: graceful-and-budgeted
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o wide && kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=status-api
